questions.question: Which selector targets all elements with the class 'highlight'?
questions.explanation: A period (.) before a name creates a class selector. .highlight selects all elements with class="highlight".

questions.question: What does the selector `div > p` target?
choices:
Only

elements that are direct children of a

All

elements that are siblings of a
questions.explanation:
The child combinator (>) selects only direct children. div > p targets

elements that are immediate children of a

, not nested deeper.

questions.question: Which selector selects every element on the page?
questions.explanation: The universal selector (*) matches every element in the document. It is often used for CSS resets.

questions.question: What does the `+` combinator select in `h2 + p`?
choices:
The first

element immediately after an

All

elements that come after an
questions.explanation: The adjacent sibling combinator (+) selects the element that is immediately after the first element, at the same nesting level.

questions.question: The ___ character is used as a prefix for ID selectors in CSS.
questions.explanation: The hash symbol (#) is used to select an element by its ID, e.g., #header selects the element with id="header".

questions.question: The ___ combinator selects all subsequent siblings, written as h2 ~ p.
questions.explanation: The general sibling combinator (~) selects all sibling elements that follow the first element, unlike + which only selects the immediately adjacent one.
